---
page-layout: full
pagetitle: "Ian Phillips"
---

::: {.home-header}

![](images/ian-phillips-web.jpg){.portrait}

# Ian Phillips

::: {.subtitle}
Bloomberg Distinguished Professor of Philosophy and Brain Sciences  
Johns Hopkins University
:::

::: {.profile-links}
[![](images/icons/google-scholar.svg){.profile-icon} Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/) [![](images/icons/researchgate.svg){.profile-icon} ResearchGate](https://www.researchgate.net/) [![](images/icons/bluesky.svg){.profile-icon} Bluesky](https://bsky.app/) [CV](cv.qmd)
:::

:::

::: {.home-main}

I am jointly appointed in the William H. Miller III Department of Philosophy and the Department of Psychological and Brain Sciences. I am also a regular visiting professor at Oxford University.

My work lies at the intersection of philosophy and the mind/brain sciences, with particular interests in perception, memory, imagination, consciousness, and temporal experience. I am also writing a biography of Gareth Evans.

## Recent updates

- “Response duration tracks confidence,” in press at *PNAS*.
- “Pretending not to know,” published in *Psychological Science*.
- “Event-based warping,” published in *Journal of Experimental Psychology: General*.
- “Aphantasia reimagined,” published in *Noûs*.

:::
